references.text: Matthew, KM. 1976. A revision of the genus Mastixia (Cornaceae). Blumea 23: 51-93
Matthew, KM. 1977. Cornaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 8: 85-97

genera.last_edited: Last edited by John Paul Collantes & Pieter B. Pelser, 1 June 2020

species.citation: K.M.Matthew , Blumea 23 (1976) 75 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 94; Type: from Sarawak.
species.notes: Known from Rizal from Montalban; BS 12389 Loher, UC. Lowland forest.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 654 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 95. ssp. philippinensis (Wangerin) K.M.Matthew, Blumea 23 (1976) 81; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 96; ≡ Mastixia philippinensis Wangerin, Repert. Spec. Nov. Regni Veg. 10 (1912) 273; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 241. = Mastixia subcaudata Merr., PJS 13c (1918) 43; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 242. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Camarines Sur, Laguna, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Misamis Occidental, Surigao del Sur, PALAWAN. Notes: Throughout the Philippines. Lowland forests up to 1350m. Fl. May-Sep; fr. Jan-Dec. Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Malaysia, Moluccas, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. CEBU, LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Camarines Sur, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya (photos), Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Lanao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Surigao del Sur, PALAWAN.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 42 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 242; Matthew, Blumea 23 (1976) 76; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 95; Type: BS 28575 Ramos & Edaño (PNH, holo, lost; BO, K, NY, P, US, iso). = Mastixia crassifolia Merr. , PJS 26 (1925) 486 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 250; Type: BS 40377 Ramos & Edaño (PNH, holo, lost; A, BO, K, L, P, US, iso). = Mastixia pachyphylla Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 325 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 241; Type: BS 30399 Ramos (PNH, holo, lost; BO, NY, P, US, iso).
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CATANDUANES, LUZON: Benguet, Quezon, ?MINDANAO: ?Lanao del Norte, ?Misamis Occidental.
species.notes: Lowland to montane primary forests up to 2300m. Fl. Feb-Mar; fr. Sep-Feb.

species.citation: Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 655 ; Matthew, Blumea 23 (1976) 68; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 92. var. clarkeana (King) Danser, Blumea 1 (1934) 62; Matthew, Blumea 23 (1976) 72; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 8 (1977) 93; ≡ Mastixia clarkeana King, J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 71 (1902) 75. = Mastixia premnoides (Elmer) Hallier f., Beih. Bot. Centralbl., 2nd Abt. 34 (1916) 41; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 242; ≡ Vitex premnoides Elmer, LPB 8 (1915) 2874; Bramley, Fl. Males. ser 1, 23 (2019) 421; Type: Elmer 11644 (PNH, holo, lost; K, L, NY, US, iso), Mindanao: Todaya (Mt Apo). Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur. Notes: Low elevation to 1100m. Native.
species.distribution: MINDANAO: Davao, Davao del Sur, MINDORO.
Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, ?Philippines, Sumatra. ?SAMAR.
species.notes: This species is potentially represented in the Philippines by Gutierrez et al. 433 (PNH 117414) in L from Samar, Mt Apoy, Llorente, May/Jun-1969.
